data.title: The whale's 7 siblings have spent 10 million USDC to buy 2,664 ETH
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, a mysterious whale/institution, 7Siblings, borrowed 20 million USDC and used 10 million USDC to purchase 2,664 ETH at $3,754. They currently hold approximately 10 million USDC and may be preparing to increase their holdings.

data.title: Binance HODLer Airdrop Launches ZEROBASE (ZBT), Trading Opens on October 17th
data.description: According to an official announcement, Binance will list ZEROBASE (ZBT) at 9:00 PM (Beijing Time) on October 17, 2025, with trading available for ZBT/USDT, ZBT/USDC, ZBT/BNB, ZBT/FDUSD, and ZBT/TRY. Additionally, Binance will offer a ZBT airdrop reward to users who subscribe to the BNB Earn product between 8:00 AM (Beijing Time) on October 11, 2025, and 7:59 AM (Beijing Time) on October 14, 2025. Details of the airdrop are expected to be announced within 24 hours, and the new tokens will be distributed to users' spot wallets at least one hour before trading begins. (This newsletter was generated with AI assistance.)

data.title: Global Gold Market Cap Surpasses $30 Trillion, Outshining Major Companies
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the global gold market has reached a historic milestone, with its total market capitalization surpassing $30 trillion. This achievement places gold at the top of the global market, exceeding the combined market value of leading companies such as Apple, Microsoft, and Nvidia. The valuation is based on the World Gold Council's reported 216,265 tons of mined gold and current gold prices, reaffirming gold's status as a safe haven and store of value.

In contrast, Bitcoin, often referred to as 'digital gold,' currently has a market cap of approximately $2 trillion. To match gold's market value, the price of a single Bitcoin would need to rise to about $1.5 million, more than ten times its current value. Cathie Wood has previously predicted that Bitcoin's price could exceed $1 million by 2030.

Analysts from BiyaPay highlight that gold symbolizes the 'trust anchor' of the traditional financial system, while Bitcoin represents the new belief in the era of decentralization. With trends such as de-dollarization, shifts in global liquidity, and accelerated institutional entry, the boundaries between traditional and digital assets are becoming increasingly blurred. In an extremely bullish scenario, if market conditions are exceptionally favorable, Bitcoin could potentially reach $1.5 million per coin between 2035 and 2040.

BiyaPay offers investors a global platform to invest in both gold and digital assets, supporting the seamless conversion of multiple fiat and digital currencies with transparent real-time exchange rates. The platform provides a 'zero-fee' trading experience for spot and contract transactions. Additionally, BiyaPay allows users to invest directly in U.S. stocks, Hong Kong stocks, futures, and gold using USDT, enabling easy navigation between traditional and emerging markets on a single platform.

data.title: In the Age of Surveillance, Privacy Coins Are Having a Moment
data.description:
Amid a turbulent market and increased regulatory scrutiny, privacy tokens are outperforming major cryptocurrencies—surging on renewed investor interest, rising search trends, and growing concerns over data security.
source: https://www.blockhead.co/2025/10/17/in-the-age-of-surveillance-privacy-coins-are-having-a-moment/

data.title: Japan’s Megabanks Unite to Launch Yen- and Dollar-Pegged Stablecoins
data.description:
Japan’s top three banks team up to issue fiat-pegged stablecoins, starting with the yen, aiming to streamline settlements and modernize corporate payments.
source: https://coincu.com/news/japans-megabanks-unite-to-launch-yen/

data.title: Swiss Regulator Files Criminal Complaint Against FIFA Over Blockchain Token Sales
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Swiss gambling regulator Gespa has filed a criminal complaint against FIFA following an initial investigation into blockchain token sales related to World Cup tickets. Gespa, which also oversees lotteries and sports betting, stated on its website that the FIFA Collect platform constitutes an unlicensed gambling service in Switzerland, thereby rendering it illegal.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Whale Faces Liquidation Amid Bitcoin Decline
data.description: According to Foresight News, blockchain security firm PeckShield has reported that a significant cryptocurrency holder, known as a whale, experienced a liquidation event. The whale had leveraged positions in Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC), Ethereum (ETH), and Chainlink (LINK) through the Aave platform. This liquidation occurred after Bitcoin's price fell below $104,000, resulting in a loss of $1.45 million.

source: https://coincu.com/news/orderly-perpetual-rwa-trading-defi/

data.title: Crypto Showdown: Fetch.ai and Ocean Protocol Clash Over $84M in Tokens
data.description:
Fetch. ai CEO Humayun Sheikh has publicly accused the Ocean Protocol Foundation of mismanaging 286 million FET tokens valued at about $84 million, a dispute that has sparked legal threats and drawn a response from cryptocurrency exchange Binance. Key points: The dispute traces back to the formation of the Artificial Superintelligence (ASI) Alliance in 2024, an initiative that unified three leading AI-driven blockchain projects, Fetch. ai, Ocean Protocol, and SingularityNET, under a single token structure aimed at advancing decentralized artificial intelligence collaboration
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/10/17/crypto-showdown-fetch-ai-and-ocean-protocol-clash-over-84m-in-tokens/

data.title: Bank of England Sets ‘Temporary’ Stablecoin Caps — With No End in Sight
data.description:
The Bank of England will impose temporary stablecoin caps, £10K–£20K for individuals and £10M for businesses, to protect credit, with no set timeline for lifting them. The post Bank of England Sets ‘Temporary’ Stablecoin Caps — With No End in Sight first appeared on The Shib Daily.
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/10/17/bank-of-england-sets-temporary-stablecoin-caps-with-no-end-in-sight/

data.title: Trump Confirms US Is in a Trade War With China — Bitcoin Feels the Sting
data.description:
President Donald Trump confirmed that the U. S. is now in an active trade war with China after threatening 100% tariffs on Chinese imports. The announcement, sparked by China’s tightened export controls on rare earth minerals, triggered a steep Bitcoin sell-off and renewed global market volatility
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/10/17/trump-confirms-us-is-in-a-trade-war-with-china-bitcoin-feels-the-sting/

data.title: Japan's Mitsubishi UFJ Bank, Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, and Mizuho Bank will jointly issue a stablecoin
data.description: According to Nikkei, major Japanese banks will issue stablecoins for commercial use. Mitsubishi UFJ Bank, Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, and Mizuho Bank will jointly issue a stablecoin.
